layout_and_spacing.hero_section: 100vh full-screen pinned section using GSAP ScrollTrigger to capture vertical scroll and convert it to horizontal timeline animations.
layout_and_spacing.grid: Absolute positioning inside the pinned relative container for precision tracking.
layout_and_spacing.padding: Generous spacing, floating text boxes with p-6 or p-8.

components.track_container: A 100vh relative container that gets pinned by GSAP. Inside, it holds the headline, arrow, car, and impact metrics.
components.headline: Centered horizontally and vertically, huge text 'W E L C O M E  I T Z  F I Z Z'. Split into individual span elements per letter/word for granular animation.
components.fire_arrow: An absolute positioned . Starts off-screen left. Moves across the screen horizontally.
components.car_image: The McLaren top-view image. Moves horizontally in tandem with the arrow, perhaps slightly offset or leading/trailing.
components.impact_metrics: Four absolute positioned glassmorphism cards (top-left, bottom-left, top-right, bottom-right). They contain stats like '58% Increase' etc.

animations.initial_load: Headline letters fade in slightly (or remain hidden until scroll), background is pure black. Initial fade in for the car and arrow.
animations.scroll_timeline:
A GSAP timeline with `ScrollTrigger` (scrub: true, pin: true). As user scrolls down:
1. Arrow and Car translate from `left: 0%` to `left: 100%`.
2. Headline letters stagger fade in (opacity 0 -> 1) exactly when the arrow passes over them.
3. Impact metrics fade in and translate up (`y: 50 -> 0`) at various scroll progress points (e.g. 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%).
4. Reversing scroll seamlessly runs the timeline backwards (arrow moves right-to-left, hiding words).

instructions_to_main_agent: Use pure `#000000` background so the fire arrow images seamlessly blend in.
Apply `mix-blend-mode: screen` or `lighten` to the arrow images just in case there are slight compression artifacts in the black background of the JPEG.
Implement the pinning scroll effect: The container is pinned, and the scroll distance dictates the progress of a GSAP timeline.
Map the X-axis translation of the arrow to the ScrollTrigger progress. Simultaneously, map the opacity of the letters to the arrow's X position (or just use a timeline with staggered reveals).
Use the generated car top-view image and make it translate alongside the arrow. Use CSS `filter: drop-shadow(...)` for a cool glow effect on the car.
Add `data-testid` to all semantic parts: pinned-section, main-headline, fire-arrow, mclaren-car, impact-metric-1, etc.
